tank_mix_guidance: During periods of dollar spot pressure, the label says to always mix Heritage with Daconil, Banner Maxx, Secure, or another dollar spot control fungicide.
The label says Heritage is compatible in tank mixes with many other fungicides that control dollar spot.
Do not tank-mix Heritage with other products unless local experience indicates the tank mix is safe and effective under your conditions.
azoxystrobin.irrigation_guidance: For labeled fairy ring use, add the recommended wetting-agent rate and water in immediately with 1/8 to 1/4 inches of irrigation.
azoxystrobin.rainfast: Runoff of this product will be reduced by avoiding applications when rainfall or irrigation is expected to occur within 48 hours.
azoxystrobin.max_apps_per_year: Do not make more than eight applications of Heritage SC per acre per year.

Provide sufficient jet or mechanical agitation during filling and application to keep the tank mix uniformly suspended.
Tank Mixes For broader spectrum control, Banner MAXX II can be tank-mixed with other fungicides.
propiconazole.max_apps_per_year: Do not apply more than 5.4 gallons of Banner MAXX II/acre/calendar year.
propiconazole.max_rate_per_app: The stored Banner MAXX turf disease table tops out at 4 fl oz per 1000 sq ft per application.
propiconazole.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured Banner MAXX label record.
propiconazole.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Banner MAXX label record.
propiconazole.application_window_notes: Apply after mowing OR allow sprayed area to completely dry before mowing.

azoxystrobin_propiconazole.rainfast: No single rainfast interval is stated in the structured Headway record; match the formulation-specific disease table when rainfall or post-application irrigation timing matters.
tank_mix_guidance: Use tank mixtures with fungicides from a different group that are equally effective on the target pest when such use is permitted.
Use at least the minimum application rate as labeled by the manufacturer when tank mixing for resistance management.
For active dollar spot pressure on the stored Headway G label, pair the program with Daconil (chlorothalonil) where the disease table calls for it.
azoxystrobin_propiconazole.max_apps_per_year: Match the formulation-specific Headway label and disease table for the annual program limit; no single universal annual maximum is stored across both Headway formulations in this KB record.
azoxystrobin_propiconazole.max_rate_per_app: Match the specific Headway or Headway G disease table because the liquid and granular formulations use different per-application rates.

azoxystrobin_propiconazole.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Headway record.
azoxystrobin_propiconazole.application_window_notes: For the stored Headway G spring dead spot uses, apply 1/4 to 1/2 inch of irrigation directly after application and follow the disease table for 14- or 28-day program timing.

site_restrictions: Golf course turfgrasses only.

azoxystrobin_difenoconazole.retreatment_interval: The stored Briskway disease programs commonly use 14- to 28-day intervals depending on target and pressure; dollar spot programs can tighten to 14- to 21-day timing. Match the specific disease table before retreating.
azoxystrobin_difenoconazole.irrigation_guidance: Do not apply Briskway through any type of irrigation system. For foliar diseases, allow Briskway to dry completely before irrigation. For soil-borne diseases, Briskway can be watered in after application.
azoxystrobin_difenoconazole.rainfast: Runoff of this product will be reduced by avoiding applications when rainfall or irrigation is expected to occur within 48 hours.
tank_mix_guidance: Use tank mixtures with fungicides from a different group that are equally effective on the target pest when such use is permitted.

Do not combine Briskway in the spray tank with pesticides, surfactants, or fertilizers unless compatibility charts or your own prior use shows the combination is physically compatible, effective, and non-injurious.
For dollar spot, alternate Briskway with contact fungicides such as chlorothalonil (for example Daconil-branded products).
azoxystrobin_difenoconazole.max_apps_per_year: Do not apply more than 1.21 gallons (156 fl oz) of Briskway per acre per year, equivalent to 3.6 fl oz per 1000 square feet per year.
azoxystrobin_difenoconazole.max_rate_per_app: The stored Briskway turf disease table tops out at 1.2 fl oz per 1000 sq ft (52 fl oz per acre) per application.
azoxystrobin_difenoconazole.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured Briskway record.
azoxystrobin_difenoconazole.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Briskway record.
azoxystrobin_difenoconazole.application_window_notes: Apply preventatively when conditions are favorable for disease. For fairy ring, apply early in spring before symptoms and irrigate into the thatch before the spray dries.

rates.standard: 3.5-14.0 fl oz/acre (0.08-0.32 fl oz/1000 sq ft), depending on disease target

site_restrictions: Golf course turfgrasses only.
pydiflumetofen.rei: Do not enter until sprays have dried.
pydiflumetofen.retreatment_interval: The stored Posterity disease programs commonly use 14- to 28-day intervals depending on target. Dollar spot, fairy ring, and Microdochium patch programs commonly run on 21- to 28-day timing, while spring dead spot programs can tighten to 14- to 28-day timing. Do not make more than 2 sequential applications before alternating away from Group 7.
pydiflumetofen.irrigation_guidance: Do not apply Posterity through irrigation systems. Allow Posterity to completely dry prior to irrigating. For fairy ring, add the recommended rate of a wetting agent and irrigate the product into the thatch before the spray dries.
pydiflumetofen.rainfast: Runoff of this product will be reduced by avoiding applications when rainfall or irrigation is expected to occur within 48 hours.
tank_mix_guidance: Use tank mixtures with fungicides from a different group that are equally effective on the target pest when such use is permitted.

Do not combine Posterity in the spray tank with pesticides, surfactants, or fertilizers unless compatibility charts or your own prior use show the combination is physically compatible, effective, and non-injurious.
For broad-spectrum disease control, the label allows Posterity to be tank-mixed or alternated with a contact fungicide containing chlorothalonil and acibenzolar-S-methyl, such as Daconil Action.
pydiflumetofen.max_apps_per_year: Do not apply more than 28.0 fl oz/acre/year of Posterity or other pydiflumetofen-containing products, and do not make more than 2 applications at the maximum rate per year.
pydiflumetofen.max_rate_per_app: Do not exceed 14.0 fl oz/acre (0.32 fl oz/1000 sq ft) per application.
pydiflumetofen.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured Posterity record.
pydiflumetofen.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Posterity record.
pydiflumetofen.application_window_notes: Apply preventatively when conditions become favorable for disease. For fairy ring, start early in spring before symptoms, add a wetting agent, and irrigate into the thatch before the spray dries.

rates.standard: 1-2 fl oz/1000 sq ft for most turf diseases, depending on target and pressure

site_restrictions: Golf courses, sod farms, sports fields, lawns, parks, municipal grounds, and cemeteries.
Application to golf course tees, greens, fairways, and roughs is permitted only if the turf is 2.5 inches or less in height.
trifloxystrobin_triadimefon.rei: Do not enter until sprays have dried. Agricultural REI is 12 hours.
trifloxystrobin_triadimefon.retreatment_interval: The stored Tartan Stressgard programs commonly use 14- to 28-day intervals for most diseases, with southern blight repeating every 14 days, take-all patch on 28-day timing, and stripe smut on a 3-application seasonal program. Do not apply more than 2 sequential Tartan applications for gray leaf spot or more than 3 sequential applications for other diseases before alternating away from the same mode-of-action pattern.
trifloxystrobin_triadimefon.irrigation_guidance: Do not apply Tartan through irrigation systems. For control of both foliar and soil-borne diseases, allow the sprayed area to completely dry before irrigation. Fairy ring preventive applications must be watered in with 1/8 to 1/4 inch of irrigation within 24 hours.
trifloxystrobin_triadimefon.rainfast: No explicit rainfast interval is stated in the structured Tartan record; the label instead says to allow the sprayed area to completely dry before irrigation.
tank_mix_guidance: Tartan can be tank mixed with other fungicides for broader-spectrum control, but compatibility should be checked before tank mixing.
The label says Tartan is compatible with most insecticide, fungicide, and foliar nutrient products, but tank mix partners should still be tested first.
Follow the most restrictive directions, limitations, and precautionary statements from every product in the tank mix.
trifloxystrobin_triadimefon.max_apps_per_year: For turfgrass, up to 345 fl oz of Tartan Stressgard can be applied per acre per year.
trifloxystrobin_triadimefon.max_rate_per_app: The stored Tartan turf disease table tops out at 2 fl oz per 1000 sq ft per application for listed diseases.
trifloxystrobin_triadimefon.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured Tartan record.
trifloxystrobin_triadimefon.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Tartan record.
trifloxystrobin_triadimefon.application_window_notes: Apply after mowing or allow the sprayed area to completely dry before mowing. For fairy ring, make the first preventive application in winter or spring and a second application 28 days later, and stop plant growth regulators one week before and after the Tartan applications.

trifloxystrobin.note: Broad-spectrum strobilurin fungicide for foliar, stem, and root diseases, with dollar spot positioned as suppression unless a partner is added under pressure.
site_restrictions: May be applied to golf courses, institutional, commercial and residential lawns, sod farms, sports fields, parks, municipal grounds, and cemeteries.
trifloxystrobin.rei: Do not enter or allow worker entry into treated areas during the restricted-entry interval of 12 hours.
trifloxystrobin.retreatment_interval: The stored Compass programs commonly use 14- to 21-day intervals, with some curative or lower-pressure foliar programs stretching to 21 to 28 days. The label also limits gray leaf spot programs to no more than 2 sequential Compass applications and all other diseases to no more than 3 sequential Compass applications before rotation.
trifloxystrobin.irrigation_guidance: Do not apply through any type of irrigation system. For soil-borne diseases, use enough carrier to reach the crown and upper root zone, and allow the sprayed area to completely dry before irrigation.
trifloxystrobin.rainfast: No explicit rainfast interval is stated in the structured Compass record; the label instead says to allow the sprayed area to completely dry before irrigation.
tank_mix_guidance: For longer and broader control including dollar spot, Compass can be tank mixed with other fungicides after a compatibility check.
The label repeatedly pairs Compass with Bayleton 50 for broader dollar spot, anthracnose, summer patch, rust, Fusarium patch, and pink snow mold programs.
When conditions favor Pythium blight, the label says to mix a Pythium-active fungicide with Compass.
trifloxystrobin.max_apps_per_year: For turfgrass, do not apply more than 34.5 ounces of Compass Fungicide per acre per year.
trifloxystrobin.max_rate_per_app: The stored Compass turf disease tables top out at 0.25 oz per 1000 sq ft per application.
trifloxystrobin.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured Compass label record.
trifloxystrobin.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Compass label record.
trifloxystrobin.application_window_notes: Apply after mowing or allow the sprayed area to completely dry before mowing. Under high disease pressure, use the higher rate and the shorter interval.

Do not combine Daconil Action with Dipel, Latron B-1956, Latron AG-98, horticultural oil, or products containing xylene.
A tank mix of Daconil Action with Chipco Signature can result in physical antagonism if not mixed properly.

If tank mixes are desired, read and follow all directions, precautions, and limitations on labeling of all products used.
Tank mixtures or other applications of products referenced on this label are permitted only in those states in which the referenced products are registered.

fluazinam.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Secure label record.

tank_mix_guidance: 3.1 Plant Safety NOTICE TO USER: Due to the large number of species and varieties of ornamentals and nursery plants, it is impossible to test every one for tolerance to Subdue MAXX and tank mixtures with Subdue MAXX.
The applicator must determine if Subdue MAXX and tank mixtures with other fungicides can be used safely prior to commercial use.
Use tank mixtures with fungicides from a different group that are equally effective on the target pest when such use is permitted.

pyraclostrobin.irrigation_guidance: 5 Use Precautions for Sprinkler Irrigation Application DO NOT connect an irrigation system (including greenhouse systems) used for pesticide application to a public water system unless the pesticide label-prescribed safety devices for public water systems are in place.
pyraclostrobin.rainfast: Runoff of this product will be reduced by avoiding applications when rainfall is forecast to occur within 48 hours.
tank_mix_guidance: Apply Insignia SC solo or in tank mixes with other registered fungicides.
To improve control of certain diseases, Insignia SC can be tank mixed with other effective (nonstrobilurin) fungicides.

Compatibility Test for Tank Mix Components Resistance Management Advisory The following instructions can delay the development of fungicide resistance: Add components in the following sequence using 2 teaspoons for each pound or 1 teaspoon for each pint of label rate per acre.
pyraclostrobin.max_apps_per_year: Maximum seasonal use rate: do not apply more than a total of 4.4 fl oz of Insignia SC per 1000 sq ft per year (13.37 pounds or 1.5 gallons of Insignia SC per acre per year).

Note: Products containing thiabendazole or other products containing thiophanate-methyl are not appropriate for rotation or use in tank mixtures with this product as they are of similar chemistry and will contribute to the development of resistance.
Tank Mixing Instructions: It is the pesticide user’s responsibility to ensure that all products included in the tank mix are registered for the intended use.
thiophanate_methyl.max_apps_per_year: For all foliar applications to ornamentals, do not apply more than 75 gallons product/acre per year (300 lb a.i./acre/year).
thiophanate_methyl.max_rate_per_app: FOLIAR APPLICATION Restrictions For all ornamentals except cut ﬂowers, do not exceed a maximum single application rate of 96 ﬂ oz product/acre (3.0 lb a.i./acre).

thiophanate_methyl.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured 3336 label record.
thiophanate_methyl.application_window_notes: For best results, use spray mixture the same day it is prepared.

fludioxonil.retreatment_interval: Wait a minimum of 7 days before retreating with Medallion.
fludioxonil.irrigation_guidance: For control of foliar diseases, allow sprayed area to completely dry before irrigation.
fludioxonil.rainfast: For best results, avoid application when rainfall is forecast in the next 48 hours.
tank_mix_guidance: Use tank mixtures with a fungicide from a different group that are equally effective on the target pest when such use is permitted.
Medallion SC + Tank Mixtures: To determine the physical compatibility of Medallion SC with other products, use a jar test, as described below.
If using Medallion SC in a tank mixture, observe all directions for use, use rates, dilution ratios, precautions, and limitations which appear on the tank-mix product label.
fludioxonil.max_apps_per_year: Do not apply more than 6 fluid ounces of fludioxonil per 1,000 sq ft per year on turfgrass.
fludioxonil.max_rate_per_app: The stored Medallion turf disease table tops out at 2.0 fl oz per 1000 sq ft per application.
fludioxonil.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured Medallion label record.
fludioxonil.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Medallion label record.
fludioxonil.application_window_notes: Begin applications prior to disease development. Brown ring patch and Rhizoctonia leaf and sheath spot programs can repeat on 7- to 14-day timing; for broader control up to 21 days, the label references tank-mixing with Heritage.

iprodione.retreatment_interval: No single fixed retreatment interval is stated in the structured 26GT turf record. Match the disease table and site directions before reapplying.
iprodione.irrigation_guidance: DIRECTIONS THROUGH SPRINKLER IRRIGATION SYSTEMS Do not use through sprinkler irrigation systems in California.
iprodione.rainfast: No explicit rainfast interval is stated in the structured Banol label record.
tank_mix_guidance: TANK MIXTURES ADDITIONAL DISEASE CONTROL If turf is threatened by additional diseases, Chipco 26019 FLO brand Fungicide is compatible with most commonly used fungicides such as ProStar, Compass, and Heritage.
If a tank mixture is used, follow label directions for the use of that product.

Broad Spectrum Disease Control and Resistance Management: A tank mixture of Chipco 26019 FLO brand Fungicide and Cleary’s 3336 F provides effective, broad spectrum turf disease control and also serves as a useful tank mixture in the resistance management program required for other resistance sensitive fungicides.
iprodione.max_apps_per_year: Do not make more than 6 applications per year.
iprodione.max_rate_per_app: RESTRICTIONS: Do not apply more than 2.5 (1.25 lbs a.i./A) quarts product/acre per application.
iprodione.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured 26GT label record.
iprodione.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured 26GT label record.

propamocarb.reseeding_interval: OVERSEEDED AREAS: Apply after germination to control Curative Treatment* Pythium Damping-Off.
propamocarb.overseeding_interval: OVERSEEDED AREAS: Apply after germination to control Curative Treatment* Pythium Damping-Off.
propamocarb.application_window_notes: OVERSEEDED AREAS: Apply after germination to control Curative Treatment* Pythium Damping-Off.

fosetyl_al.retreatment_interval: No explicit fixed retreatment interval is stated in the structured Signature label record; follow the labeled disease program and seasonal maximum for the site.
fosetyl_al.irrigation_guidance: DIRECTIONS THROUGH SPRINKLER IRRIGATION SYSTEMS Apply this product only through sprinkler irrigation systems including microjet, drip, solid set, and center pivot.
fosetyl_al.rainfast: No explicit rainfast interval is stated in the structured Signature label record.
tank_mix_guidance: To delay fungicide resistance, take one or more of the following steps: Use tank mixtures with fungicide from a different group that are equally effective on the target pest when such use is permitted.

TURF TANK MIXTURES A CHIPCO SIGNATURE FUNGICIDE program encourages the development of a thick, vigorous turf stand that is better able to tolerate various stresses (heat, humidity, drying winds, traffic etc.) that can have a negative effect on turf health.
432-1223) Mixing Instructions Add CHIPCO SIGNATURE FUNGICIDE to the tank after the tank mix partner has been added and allowed to fully disperse.
fosetyl_al.max_apps_per_year: Do not exceed a total of 180 lb of product per acre per year.
fosetyl_al.max_rate_per_app: No explicit single-application maximum is stated in the structured Signature label record beyond the labeled use-rate directions and annual maximum.
fosetyl_al.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured Signature label record.
fosetyl_al.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Signature label record.
fosetyl_al.application_window_notes: No explicit mowing or dry-down timing note is stated in the structured Signature label record.

phosphorous_acid_salts.note: Systemic phosphorous-acid turf fungicide and plant-defense booster positioned around Pythium suppression and drought-tolerance support, not a broad-spectrum foliar fungicide.
site_restrictions: Sod farms, golf courses, sports fields, parks, residential sites, and commercial sites.
phosphorous_acid_salts.rei: Do not enter treated areas without protective clothing until sprays have dried. Agricultural REI is 4 hours.
phosphorous_acid_salts.retreatment_interval: The stored Fiata programs commonly use 14- to 28-day intervals as needed for both Pythium suppression and drought-tolerance support.
phosphorous_acid_salts.irrigation_guidance: Fiata must be applied as a foliar spray. Do not mow or irrigate the treated area until sprays have completely dried.
phosphorous_acid_salts.rainfast: No explicit rainfast interval is stated in the structured Fiata record; the label instead says not to mow or irrigate until sprays have completely dried.
tank_mix_guidance: Fiata is compatible with most products used on turf, but combinations should not be used without first checking jar compatibility and turf safety.
Certain foliar fertilizers and copper products may not be compatible with Fiata and may cause phytotoxicity.
The label includes prescribed stress/disease tank-mix pairings with Tartan, Interface, Bayleton, and Mirage StressGard, and notes trinexapac-ethyl products such as Primo MAXX in that program context.
phosphorous_acid_salts.max_apps_per_year: No single annual maximum is stated in the structured Fiata record; follow the labeled 14- to 28-day program and the product-specific use directions for the site.
phosphorous_acid_salts.max_rate_per_app: The stored Fiata turf disease and drought-tolerance programs top out at 15 fl oz per 1000 sq ft per application.
phosphorous_acid_salts.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured Fiata label record.
phosphorous_acid_salts.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Fiata label record.
phosphorous_acid_salts.application_window_notes: Use Fiata as a foliar spray. For Pythium suppression, begin preventive applications when conditions first favor disease. For drought tolerance, begin before adverse weather or watering restrictions.

rates.standard: 2.75-11.0 fl oz/1000 sq ft, depending on disease target

site_restrictions: Golf courses, sod farms, lawns around commercial and industrial buildings, and professional and collegiate athletic fields.
Do not use on home lawns or turf associated with schools, playgrounds, daycare centers, campgrounds, churches, or theme parks.
chlorothalonil_propiconazole_fludioxonil.rei: Do not enter until the treated area is dry. Agricultural REI is 12 hours.
chlorothalonil_propiconazole_fludioxonil.retreatment_interval: The stored Instrata programs commonly use 14- to 28-day intervals depending on target. Brown patch, leaf spot, and red thread can tighten to 14- to 21-day timing; dollar spot usually runs 21- to 28-day timing; summer patch can be run at 6.0 fl oz on a 14-day schedule or 11.0 fl oz on a 28-day schedule.
chlorothalonil_propiconazole_fludioxonil.irrigation_guidance: Apply Instrata in adequate carrier volume for the surface, and do not mow or water after treatment until spray deposited on the turf is thoroughly dry.
chlorothalonil_propiconazole_fludioxonil.rainfast: No explicit rainfast interval is stated in the structured Instrata record; the label instead says not to mow or water until the spray deposit is thoroughly dry.
tank_mix_guidance: Use tank mixtures with fungicides from a different group that are equally effective on the target pest when such use is permitted.
Do not combine Instrata in the spray tank with pesticides, surfactants, or fertilizers unless prior use has shown the combination physically compatible, effective, and noninjurious.
Do not combine Instrata with Aliette Signature, Chipco Signature, horticultural oil, Dipel, Latron B-1956, Latron AG-98, or products containing xylene.
chlorothalonil_propiconazole_fludioxonil.max_apps_per_year: Annual maximum depends on site and state restrictions. The structured record stores the broad annual cap of 15.0 gallons per acre per year (44.0 fl oz per 1000 sq ft per year) for the core golf/lawn athletic uses, with tighter New York county and lower-use-site limits also stated on the label.
chlorothalonil_propiconazole_fludioxonil.max_rate_per_app: The stored Instrata turf disease table tops out at 11.0 fl oz per 1000 sq ft per application.
chlorothalonil_propiconazole_fludioxonil.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured Instrata label record.
chlorothalonil_propiconazole_fludioxonil.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Instrata label record.
chlorothalonil_propiconazole_fludioxonil.application_window_notes: Begin applications when conditions favor disease development and repeat within the labeled interval range while pressure persists. Do not mow or water after treatment until the spray deposit is thoroughly dry.

chlorothalonil_propiconazole.note: Broad-spectrum premix fungicide that combines chlorothalonil contact coverage with propiconazole systemic activity for foliar and soil-borne turf disease programs.
site_restrictions: Do not use Concert II on fine fescue turf due to phytotoxicity risk.
chlorothalonil_propiconazole.rei: Do not enter or allow worker entry into treated areas during the restricted-entry interval of 12 hours.
chlorothalonil_propiconazole.retreatment_interval: The minimum retreatment interval is 14 days. Stored Concert II programs commonly use 14-day timing under higher pressure, 14- to 21-day timing on many foliar programs, and 14- to 28-day timing on broader preventative programs such as snow mold, powdery mildew, rust, and certain patch diseases.
chlorothalonil_propiconazole.irrigation_guidance: Do not apply through any type of irrigation system. Do not water for 24 hours after treatment. For control of foliar diseases, allow the sprayed area to completely dry before irrigation; for control of soil-borne diseases, Concert II can be watered in after application.
chlorothalonil_propiconazole.rainfast: No explicit rainfast interval is stated in the structured Concert II record; the label instead says not to water for 24 hours after treatment.
tank_mix_guidance: Any product packaged in water-soluble packaging should be added to the tank before Concert II and allowed to dissolve completely before other partners are added.
Concert II can be tank mixed after checking compatibility and following the most restrictive partner label.
For optimum snow mold control, the label recommends tank mixing with another snow-mold-registered fungicide.
chlorothalonil_propiconazole.max_apps_per_year: Maximum amount of Concert II per growing season: 53 fl oz per 1000 sq ft on greens, 38 fl oz per 1000 sq ft on tees, 19 fl oz per 1000 sq ft on all other turf, and 9.5 fl oz per 1000 sq ft on sod farms.
chlorothalonil_propiconazole.max_rate_per_app: Maximum single application rate of Concert II is 8.3 fl oz per 1000 sq ft.
chlorothalonil_propiconazole.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured Concert II label record.
chlorothalonil_propiconazole.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Concert II label record.
chlorothalonil_propiconazole.application_window_notes: Use Concert II in a preventative disease control program. Apply after mowing or allow the sprayed area to completely dry before mowing, and under high disease pressure use the higher rate and shorter interval.

site_restrictions: Nonresidential turfgrass only.
Golf tees, greens, fairways, roughs, and naturalized areas on golf courses; sod farms; and certain professional/collegiate athletic or commercial turf sites.
Do not use on home lawns or sensitive child-centered and school-adjacent sites called out on the label.
mefentrifluconazole_pyraclostrobin.rei: Do not enter treated areas until sprays have dried. Agricultural REI is 12 hours.
mefentrifluconazole_pyraclostrobin.retreatment_interval: The stored Navicon programs commonly use 14- to 28-day intervals depending on target, with Pythium blight able to tighten to 10- to 14-day timing, dollar spot commonly on 14 to 28 days, and the label-wide minimum re-treatment interval set at 10 days.
mefentrifluconazole_pyraclostrobin.irrigation_guidance: Do not apply through irrigation equipment. For best results on soilborne diseases, apply 0.25 inch of post-application irrigation. Fairy ring calls for 0.25 inch irrigation after application, and brown ring patch uses a short irrigation cycle immediately after treatment. Do not mow or water until the spray deposit has dried unless the disease-specific note calls for post-application irrigation.
mefentrifluconazole_pyraclostrobin.rainfast: Runoff of Navicon will be reduced by avoiding application when rainfall is forecast within 48 hours.
tank_mix_guidance: Use tank mixtures with fungicides from a different group that are equally effective on the target pest when such use is permitted.
Navicon can be tank mixed with other labeled products, but make sure each component is thoroughly mixed and suspended before adding the next partner.
When Pythium blight or severe Pythium root rot pressure exists, tank mix or rotate with a non-Group 11 fungicide labeled for that target.
mefentrifluconazole_pyraclostrobin.max_apps_per_year: Do not make more than 3 applications per acre per year, and do not apply more than 111 fl oz/acre/year (2.55 fl oz/1000 sq ft/year).
mefentrifluconazole_pyraclostrobin.max_rate_per_app: Do not apply more than 37 fl oz/acre per application (0.85 fl oz/1000 sq ft).
mefentrifluconazole_pyraclostrobin.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured Navicon label record.
mefentrifluconazole_pyraclostrobin.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Navicon label record.
mefentrifluconazole_pyraclostrobin.application_window_notes: Apply preventively when conditions favor disease. For summer patch, begin in spring when 2-inch soil temperatures reach 60°F to 65°F. For spring dead spot, apply in fall before or at the earliest sign of disease. For snow molds, make late-fall applications just before snow cover.

rates.standard: 3-7 fl oz/1000 sq ft, depending on disease target

iprodione_trifloxystrobin.note: Foliar turf fungicide positioned around dollar spot, brown patch, snow mold, and stress-season disease programs. Anthracnose support is label-framed as suppression, not a broad standalone anthracnose cure-all.
site_restrictions: Golf courses, sod farms, and institutional turf areas where fine turf is grown.
Residential sites are prohibited.
iprodione_trifloxystrobin.rei: Do not enter treated areas until sprays have dried. Agricultural REI is 24 hours.
iprodione_trifloxystrobin.retreatment_interval: The stored Interface programs commonly use 14- to 21-day intervals on greens and tees and 14- to 28-day intervals on fairways and other turf for dollar spot, brown patch, leaf spot, rapid blight, and rust. Microdochium patch repeats every 14 to 21 days, large patch can repeat in spring at 14- to 21-day timing, and Fusarium blight / necrotic ring spot preventative programs can repeat on 28-day intervals.
iprodione_trifloxystrobin.irrigation_guidance: Do not allow spray deposit to drench, and do not mow or irrigate treated areas until the foliage is completely dry. A 24-hour waiting period following treatment is preferred.
iprodione_trifloxystrobin.rainfast: No explicit rainfast interval is stated in the structured Interface record; the label instead says not to mow or irrigate until the foliage is completely dry, with a 24-hour waiting period preferred.
tank_mix_guidance: Interface is compatible with most commonly used fungicide, herbicide, insecticide, and foliar nutrient products, but tank mix safety has not been fully investigated for every combination.
Before using any tank mix not specifically identified on the label, confirm crop safety and conduct a jar test.
For Pythium blight, the label says to tank mix Interface with Chipco Signature or Banol and follow the partner label for the Pythium-active rate.
iprodione_trifloxystrobin.max_apps_per_year: Do not exceed a total of 30 fl oz of Interface per 1000 sq ft per year, and do not make more than 6 applications per year.
iprodione_trifloxystrobin.max_rate_per_app: The stored Interface turf disease table tops out at 7 fl oz per 1000 sq ft per application.
iprodione_trifloxystrobin.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured Interface label record.
iprodione_trifloxystrobin.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Interface label record.
iprodione_trifloxystrobin.application_window_notes: Begin applications when conditions favor disease development or when disease first appears unless otherwise noted. Apply one snow-mold application before lasting winter snow cover, with a second if prolonged cold, wet weather arrives before lasting snow cover.

tebuconazole.note: Golf-course-focused DMI fungicide with strong seasonal positioning around dollar spot, spring dead spot, fairy ring, large patch, summer patch, and take-all programs.
site_restrictions: Golf courses only.
Do not use on home lawns, sod for sale, or turf associated with apartments, daycare centers, playgrounds, schools, campgrounds, churches, or theme parks.
tebuconazole.rei: Do not enter treated areas until sprays have dried. Agricultural REI is 12 hours.
tebuconazole.retreatment_interval: The stored Mirage StressGard programs commonly use 14- to 28-day intervals depending on target, with Microdochium patch able to tighten to 10- to 14-day timing, dollar spot often on 21- to 28-day timing, and several soilborne targets such as necrotic ring spot, spring dead spot, take-all patch, and take-all root rot commonly on 28-day schedules.
tebuconazole.irrigation_guidance: Do not apply through irrigation systems. Make applications after mowing and allow foliage to dry thoroughly before irrigation. When the label calls for soil movement on targets like fairy ring, take-all, or root-zone diseases, lightly water the treatment into the target zone after application.
tebuconazole.rainfast: An application is rainfast after drying, approximately 30 minutes, and rain or sprinkler irrigation after that time does not decrease effectiveness.
tank_mix_guidance: Mirage StressGard may be used alone, tank mixed, or used sequentially with other registered fungicides for golf course turf, following the most restrictive label limitations.
The label says Mirage is compatible with most commonly used fungicide, herbicide, insecticide, growth regulator, and foliar nutrient products, but tank-mix safety should be confirmed first.

For added plant-health or seasonal-program positioning, the label specifically references combinations with Chipco Signature, Compass, 26GT, Interface, ProStar, and Fiata in certain disease or fairway programs.
tebuconazole.max_apps_per_year: Do not exceed 6.5 fl oz of Mirage StressGard per 1000 sq ft per year, and do not apply more than 6 applications per year.
tebuconazole.max_rate_per_app: Do not exceed 2.0 fl oz per 1000 sq ft per application overall, and do not exceed 1.0 fl oz per 1000 sq ft per application in New York State.
tebuconazole.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured Mirage label record.
tebuconazole.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Mirage label record.
tebuconazole.application_window_notes: Apply after mowing and allow foliage to dry thoroughly before irrigation. For fairy ring, water the product into the disease zone with the next irrigation cycle or within 10 hours. For spring dead spot, begin preventively in fall when 2-inch soil temperatures drop below 75 degrees F.

fluopyram_prothioconazole_propamocarb_hy.note: Root-disease-focused systemic fungicide for golf course turf that also carries suppression language for certain plant pathogenic nematodes.
site_restrictions: For control of root diseases in golf course turfgrass.
fluopyram_prothioconazole_propamocarb_hy.rei: Do not enter or allow others to enter treated areas until sprays have dried.
fluopyram_prothioconazole_propamocarb_hy.retreatment_interval: The stored Resilia programs commonly use 14- to 28-day intervals, with Pythium root rot able to tighten to 14- to 21-day timing and a 14-day minimum between applications overall.
fluopyram_prothioconazole_propamocarb_hy.irrigation_guidance: Do not apply through any type of irrigation equipment. Adequately water Resilia into the depth of the root zone or target pest after application, and allow foliage to dry before mowing.
fluopyram_prothioconazole_propamocarb_hy.rainfast: Runoff risk is reduced by avoiding applications when rainfall or irrigation is expected within 48 hours.
tank_mix_guidance: Resilia can be tank mixed with other labeled products if the full combination is legal on the site and the most restrictive partner label is followed.
The label includes jar-test procedures and says to confirm compatibility before making a new tank mix.
When water-soluble packaging is part of the mix, disperse those products fully before adding other tank-mix partners.
fluopyram_prothioconazole_propamocarb_hy.max_apps_per_year: Do not exceed 16 fl oz of Resilia per 1000 sq ft per year, and do not make more than 4 applications per year at the maximum single rate.
fluopyram_prothioconazole_propamocarb_hy.max_rate_per_app: Do not exceed 4 fl oz of Resilia per 1000 sq ft per application.
fluopyram_prothioconazole_propamocarb_hy.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured Resilia label record.
fluopyram_prothioconazole_propamocarb_hy.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Resilia label record.
fluopyram_prothioconazole_propamocarb_hy.application_window_notes: Apply preventively when conditions favor root-disease activity, then water the treatment into the target root zone and use the shorter interval when pressure is high.

boscalid.rei: No explicit restricted-entry interval is stored in the structured Emerald label record.
boscalid.retreatment_interval: No explicit fixed retreatment interval is stated in the structured Emerald label record; follow the labeled disease program and seasonal maximum for the site.
boscalid.irrigation_guidance: After application, allow foliage to dry prior to mowing or irrigation.
boscalid.rainfast: Runoff of this product will be reduced by avoiding applications when rainfall is forecast to occur within 48 hours.
tank_mix_guidance: For the control of turfgrass diseases not listed on this label, Emerald may be tank mixed with labeled rates of other fungicides.
Follow label directions of any tank mix product and apply at the specified rate based on target disease.
Emerald may be applied as a solo foliar spray or in tank mixes with other registered turfgrass fungicides.
boscalid.max_apps_per_year: Maximum seasonal use rate: do not apply more than 48 ounces of Emerald per acre per year (1.1 ounces per 1,000 sq ft per year).
boscalid.max_rate_per_app: Restrictions and Limitations Maximum seasonal use rate: DO NOT apply more than a total of 1.1 ounces of Emerald per 1000 sq ft per year (48 ounces Emerald per acre per year).
boscalid.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured Emerald label record.
boscalid.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Emerald label record.
boscalid.application_window_notes: No explicit mowing or dry-down timing note is stated in the structured Emerald label record.

prodiamine.note: Pre-emergent herbicide; exact rate depends on turf species, residual length desired, and whether the application is single or sequential.
prodiamine.retreatment_interval: No fixed retreatment interval is stated in the structured Barricade record; follow the species-specific annual maximum and residual program timing.
prodiamine.irrigation_guidance: Chemigation: Do not apply this product through any type of irrigation system unless instructed otherwise in this label.
prodiamine.rainfast: No explicit rainfast interval is stated in the structured Barricade label record; activation depends on rainfall or irrigation before weed seed germination.
tank_mix_guidance: Use tank mixtures with herbicides from a different group if such use is permitted; where information on resistance in target weed species is available, use the less resistance-prone partner at a rate that will control the target weed(s) equally as well as the more resistance-prone partner.
For tank mixing instructions, refer to the section “Mixing Order for Tank Mixtures.” 6.

TANK MIXING BARRICADE 4FL Application Timing And Rate - Turfgrass Barricade 4FL may be tank mixed with certain other EPA-registered herbicides to provide a broader spectrum of weed control or to control emerged weeds.
prodiamine.max_apps_per_year: Restriction: Do not apply more than the highest rate listed for each species in a calendar year.
prodiamine.max_rate_per_app: Maximum Application Rate of Barricade 4FL Per Calendar Year by Turf Species fl oz Product/A fl oz Product/1,000 sq ft Bermudagrass2 Bahiagrass Centipedegrass Kikuyugrass Seashore Paspalum St.
prodiamine.reseeding_interval: When to Apply Barricade 4FL After Overseeding Turf Use Precautions Injury to desirable seedlings is likely if Barricade 4FL is applied before the secondary roots of seedlings are in the second inch of soil, not thatch plus soil.
prodiamine.overseeding_interval: When to Apply Barricade 4FL After Overseeding Turf Use Precautions Injury to desirable seedlings is likely if Barricade 4FL is applied before the secondary roots of seedlings are in the second inch of soil, not thatch plus soil.
prodiamine.application_window_notes: Apply after cuttings form roots and are established.

dithiopyr.retreatment_interval: No fixed retreatment interval is stated in the structured Dimension record; follow the seasonal maximum and sequential-program timing limits on the label.
dithiopyr.irrigation_guidance: Chemigation: Do not apply this product through any type of irrigation system. For preemergence residual control of crabgrass, apply at least 0.5 inch of water after application; to optimize postemergence control, delay irrigation for 6 hours after application.
dithiopyr.rainfast: For preemergence residual control of crabgrass, apply at least 0.5 inch of water after application; to optimize postemergence control, delay irrigation for 6 hours after application.
tank_mix_guidance: Then follow the mixing procedure listed below for tank mixtures.
When tank mixing, use the most restrictive label limitations for each of the products being used in the tank mix.

When tank mixing Dimension 2EW with other materials, a compatibility test (jar test) using relative proportions of the tank mix ingredients should be conducted prior to mixing ingredients in the spray tank.
dithiopyr.max_apps_per_year: Do not use more than 6 pints of Dimension 2EW per acre per year.
dithiopyr.max_rate_per_app: Do not apply more than 2 pints of Dimension 2EW per acre per application.
dithiopyr.reseeding_interval: Reseeding, overseeding, or sprigging within 3 months after a single application, or within 4 months after a sequential application program totaling more than 2 pints per acre, may inhibit establishment of desirable turfgrasses.
dithiopyr.overseeding_interval: Reseeding, overseeding, or sprigging within 3 months after a single application, or within 4 months after a sequential application program totaling more than 2 pints per acre, may inhibit establishment of desirable turfgrasses.
dithiopyr.application_window_notes: For best results, cultural practices that disturb the soil, such as verticutting and core-, spike-, or hydro-aerification, should be done before applying this product.

mesotrione.note: Safe at seeding, causes temporary bleaching
mesotrione.retreatment_interval: Wait a minimum of 14 days before retreating.
mesotrione.irrigation_guidance: DO NOT apply through any type of irrigation system.
mesotrione.rainfast: Runoff of this product will be reduced by avoiding applications when rainfall or irrigation is expected to occur within 48 hours.
tank_mix_guidance: If used preemergence in established turf, tank mixtures with preemergence herbicides including Barricade 65WG Herbicide (prodiamine; EPA Reg No.
TANK MIXTURES It is the pesticide user’s responsibility to ensure that all products are registered for the intended use.
Read and follow the applicable restrictions, limitations, and directions for use on all product labels involved in tank mixing.
mesotrione.max_apps_per_year: DO NOT apply more than 16 oz of Tenacity per acre per year (equivalent to a maximum of 0.50 lb of mesotrione per acre per year).
mesotrione.max_rate_per_app: Do not apply more than 8 fl oz of Tenacity per acre in a single application.
mesotrione.reseeding_interval: New seedings/new lawn establishment: Apply Tenacity at seeding or close to seeding for best performance. Wait until the newly germinated turf has been mowed two times or four weeks after emergence, whichever is longer, before making a postemergence application.
mesotrione.overseeding_interval: New seedings/new lawn establishment: Apply Tenacity at seeding or close to seeding for best performance. Wait until the newly germinated turf has been mowed two times or four weeks after emergence, whichever is longer, before making a postemergence application.
mesotrione.application_window_notes: For best results, add a non-ionic surfactant for postemergence applications.

halosulfuron.retreatment_interval: Wait at least 14 days before retreating and do not make more than 4 applications per year.
halosulfuron.irrigation_guidance: Do not apply this product through any type of irrigation system.
halosulfuron.rainfast: Runoff of this product will be greatly reduced by avoiding applications when rainfall or irrigation is expected to occur within 48 hours.
tank_mix_guidance: 2 Use tank mixtures with herbicides from a different group if such use is permitted; where information on resistance in target weed species is available, use the less resistance-prone partner at a rate that will control the target weed(s) equally as well as the more resistance-prone partner.
MIXING INSTRUCTIONS This product can be tank mixed with Glyphosate herbicide for application as a directed spray in landscaped or fallow areas.
Tank mix with Glyphosate herbicide in landscape and fallow areas and turf renovation.
halosulfuron.max_apps_per_year: USE RESTRICTIONS No more than 4 applications can be made per year with a minimum of 14 days between applications.
halosulfuron.max_rate_per_app: The stored SedgeHammer turf rates top out at 1 1/3 oz per acre in broadcast use or 0.9 g per 1000 sq ft in spot treatment.
halosulfuron.reseeding_interval: Treated areas can be overseeded with annual or perennial ryegrass or bermudagrass 2 weeks after application.
halosulfuron.overseeding_interval: Treated areas can be overseeded with annual or perennial ryegrass or bermudagrass 2 weeks after application.
halosulfuron.application_window_notes: USE PRECAUTIONS For best results, do not mow turf for 2 days before or 2 days after application.

glyphosate.note: NON-SELECTIVE. Only for renovation, dormant bermuda transition, spot treatment. NEVER for general weed control on active turf.
site_restrictions: Non-selective; not for general selective weed control in active turf.
glyphosate.retreatment_interval: No single fixed retreatment interval is stated in the structured Roundup turf-renovation record. Match reapplication timing to the target, regrowth, and renovation plan before spraying again.
glyphosate.irrigation_guidance: APPLICATION EQUIPMENT AND TECHNIQUES CHEMIGATION STATEMENT: Do not apply this product through any type of irrigation system.
glyphosate.rainfast: Rainfastness: Heavy rainfall soon after application may wash this product off of the foliage and a repeat application may be required for adequate control.
tank_mix_guidance: Tank Mixing: This product does not provide residual weed control.
Tank Mixing Procedure Mix labeled tank mixtures of this product with water as follows: 1.
Always predetermine the compatibility of labeled tank mixtures of this product with water carrier by mixing small proportional quantities in advance.
glyphosate.max_apps_per_year: Do not apply more than 1 quart of this product per acre for each application and no more than 3 quarts per acre per year for hooded sprayer applications.
glyphosate.max_rate_per_app: PRECAUTIONS, RESTRICTIONS: Do not apply more than 1 quart of this product per acre.
glyphosate.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured Roundup turf-renovation record.
glyphosate.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Roundup turf-renovation record.
glyphosate.application_window_notes: Spray Coverage: For best results, spray coverage should be uniform and complete.

2_4_d.note: Broadleaf herbicide ONLY. NOT a fungicide.
2_4_d.retreatment_interval: No single fixed retreatment interval is stated in the structured Trimec label record. Match the weed response and local label directions before reapplying.
2_4_d.irrigation_guidance: For optimum results: (1) turf should not be mowed for 1 to 2 days before and after application; (2) do not apply if rain is expected within 4 hours after the application, delay irrigation cycle for 24 hours.
2_4_d.rainfast: For optimum results: (1) turf should not be mowed for 1 to 2 days before and after application; (2) do not apply if rain is expected within 4 hours after the application, delay irrigation cycle for 24 hours.
tank_mix_guidance: No explicit tank-mix guidance is stored in the structured Trimec label record.
If you plan to tank-mix Trimec, confirm compatibility and follow the most restrictive label in the mix.
2_4_d.max_apps_per_year: Make no more than 2 applications per year.
2_4_d.max_rate_per_app: Ornamental Turf: Maximum single application rate is 5 pints product (1.49 lbs 2,4-D ae) per acre.
2_4_d.reseeding_interval: Reseed no sooner than three to four weeks after application of this product.
2_4_d.overseeding_interval: Reseed no sooner than three to four weeks after application of this product.
2_4_d.application_window_notes: Broadleaf herbicide ONLY. NOT a fungicide.

indaziflam.note: Longest residual pre-emergent. WARM-SEASON TURF ONLY. NOT safe on cool-season.
site_restrictions: Do not use on cool-season turf.
indaziflam.retreatment_interval: No single fixed retreatment interval is stated in the structured Specticle Flo record. Match retreatment timing to the weed-control program, soil texture, and annual-use cap before reapplying.
indaziflam.irrigation_guidance: Do not contaminate water intended for irrigation and domestic use.
indaziflam.rainfast: Runoff of this product will be reduced by avoiding applications when rainfall is forecasted to occur within 48 hours.
tank_mix_guidance: Read and follow the applicable restrictions and limitations and directions for use on all product labels involved in tank mixing.
Users must follow the most restrictive directions for use and precautionary statements of each product in the tank mixture.

Use tank mixtures with herbicides from a different group if such use is permitted; where information on resistance in target weed species is available, use the less resistance-prone partner at a rate that will control the target weed(s) equally as well as the more resistance-prone partner.
indaziflam.max_apps_per_year: Do not exceed 18.5 fl oz per acre of SPECTICLE FLO HERBICIDE for all applications within a 12 month period.
indaziflam.max_rate_per_app: PRODUCT USE RESTRICTIONS Do not exceed the maximum single application rates specified under each use.
indaziflam.reseeding_interval: Seeding, overseeding, re-seeding, sprigging, and sodding intervals are affected by the rate of SPECTICLE FLO HERBICIDE applied, rainfall, and soil texture in a given year.
indaziflam.overseeding_interval: Seeding, overseeding, re-seeding, sprigging, and sodding intervals are affected by the rate of SPECTICLE FLO HERBICIDE applied, rainfall, and soil texture in a given year.
indaziflam.application_window_notes: For best results remove existing mulch and replace mulch after an application of SPECTICLE FLO HERBICIDE.

sulfentrazone.retreatment_interval: No explicit fixed retreatment interval is stated in the structured Dismiss NXT record; follow the turf species table and do not exceed the labeled turf-specific use rates.
sulfentrazone.irrigation_guidance: Do not apply this product through any type of irrigation system. Best weed control results are obtained when no rainfall or irrigation occurs within 24 hours after application; if no rainfall or irrigation occurs within 10 days after application, irrigate with at least 0.5 inches.
sulfentrazone.rainfast: Best weed control results will be obtained when no rainfall or irrigation occurs within 24 hours after application.
tank_mix_guidance: Use tank mixtures with herbicides from a different group if such use is permitted; where information on resistance in target weed species is available, use the less resistance-prone partner at a rate that will control the target weed(s) equally as well as the more resistance-prone partner.

TANK MIXTURES COMPATIBILITY Dismiss NXT herbicide has been found to be compatible with most herbicides, fungicides, insecticides and growth regulators commonly used in turf and ornamental plant management.

However, when preparing a new tank mix conduct an appropriate compatibility test by mixing proportional amounts of all spray ingredients in a test vessel (jar) prior to tank mixing with other products.
sulfentrazone.max_apps_per_year: No single annual maximum is stated in the structured Dismiss NXT turf record; follow the turf species table and do not exceed the labeled turf-specific use rates.
sulfentrazone.max_rate_per_app: Do not exceed the application rate specified for the turf species in Table 1.
sulfentrazone.reseeding_interval: Application to reseeded, overseeded or sprigged areas: Reseeding, overseeding or sprigging of treated areas within one (1) month after application of this product could inhibit the establishment of desirable turfgrasses.
sulfentrazone.overseeding_interval: Application to reseeded, overseeded or sprigged areas: Reseeding, overseeding or sprigging of treated areas within one (1) month after application of this product could inhibit the establishment of desirable turfgrasses.
sulfentrazone.application_window_notes: Best weed control results are obtained when no rainfall or irrigation occurs within 24 hours after application; if no rainfall or irrigation occurs within 10 days after application, irrigate with at least 0.5 inches.

triclopyr.retreatment_interval: Reapply Turflon Ester Ultra at 4-week intervals.
triclopyr.irrigation_guidance: Use Restrictions Chemigation: Do not apply this product through any type of irrigation system.
triclopyr.rainfast: Do not water for 24 hours after application.
tank_mix_guidance: Use tank mixtures with herbicides from a different group if such use is permitted.
Refer to the product labels of tank mix products and follow all appropriate use directions, precautions, and limitations.
Read and follow the applicable restrictions and limitations and directions for use on all product labels involved in tank mixing.
triclopyr.max_apps_per_year: No single annual maximum is stored in the structured Turflon Ester record; follow the labeled 4-week interval, site directions, and single-application cap before building a season-long program.
triclopyr.max_rate_per_app: For spot treatments, do not apply more than 2 quarts of Turflon Ester Ultra per acre or 1.5 fl oz per 1000 sq ft in a single application.
triclopyr.reseeding_interval: Do not reseed for 3 weeks after application.
triclopyr.overseeding_interval: Prior to overseeding: Apply to fairways from 2 weeks period to overseeding to just before overseeding.
triclopyr.application_window_notes: Mow newly seeded turf 2 or 3 times before treating and do not water for 24 hours after application.

quinclorac.note: NOT SAFE on St. Augustine, centipede
site_restrictions: Do not use on St. Augustinegrass or centipedegrass.
quinclorac.retreatment_interval: Time sequential applications 14 to 21 days apart.
quinclorac.irrigation_guidance: DO NOT apply this product by air or through any type of irrigation system or equipment.
quinclorac.rainfast: No explicit rainfast interval is stated in the structured Drive XLR8 label record.
tank_mix_guidance: Use tank mixes and sequential applications with other non-Group 4 herbicides that are also effective on the target weeds.
Extended Grass Control To extend grass control, Drive XLR8 can be tank mixed with Pendulum 3.3 EC herbicide or Pendulum AquaCapTM herbicide to provide residual control of annual grasses.
Consult the respective tank mix labels for additional weeds controlled.
quinclorac.max_apps_per_year: Do not apply more than 128 fl ozs of Drive XLR8 product per acre (2.9 fl ozs per 1000 sq ft) per year or 1.5 lbs ae/A/year.
quinclorac.max_rate_per_app: DO NOT apply more than 128 ﬂuid ounces of Drive XLR8 per acre (or 2.9 ﬂuid ounces per 1000 square feet) in one year (1.5 lbs ae per acre per year).
quinclorac.reseeding_interval: Seeding/Overseeding/Sprigging The use of Drive XLR8 before or after seeding or overseeding a turfgrass area will not signiﬁcantly interfere with the turfgrass seed germination and growth of those grass types identiﬁed as tolerant or moderately tolerant in Table 1.
quinclorac.overseeding_interval: Seeding/Overseeding/Sprigging The use of Drive XLR8 before or after seeding or overseeding a turfgrass area will not signiﬁcantly interfere with the turfgrass seed germination and growth of those grass types identiﬁed as tolerant or moderately tolerant in Table 1.
quinclorac.application_window_notes: Mowing Information For best results, weeds should not be under stress from lack of water, excessive water, low fertility, mowing shock, excessive hot or cold temperatures, or injury from other herbicide applications.

fenoxaprop.note: Cool-season turf only. Do NOT apply above 85F.
site_restrictions: Do not apply above 85F per stored KB note.
fenoxaprop.retreatment_interval: Do not reapply ACCLAIM EXTRA HERBICIDE to previously treated seedling Kentucky bluegrass for at least two months.
fenoxaprop.irrigation_guidance: DO NOT APPLY this product through any type of irrigation system.
fenoxaprop.rainfast: No explicit rainfast interval is stated in the structured Acclaim Extra label record.
tank_mix_guidance: **Apply this rate only when tank mixing with pre-emergence herbicides.
Tank mixing with micronutrient mixes containing nitrogen and iron or slow release nitrogen products may alleviate injury symptoms to bentgrass turf.
DO NOT tank mix ACCLAIM EXTRA HERBICIDE with other pesticides or fertilizers on bentgrass turf except those fungicides listed on this label.
fenoxaprop.max_apps_per_year: DO NOT apply more than 10 applications per year.
fenoxaprop.max_rate_per_app: ***Do not apply more than 28 ounces of product to Kentucky bluegrass or Zoysia grass per application.
fenoxaprop.reseeding_interval: All other turf species must be overseeded after a 21-day waiting period.
fenoxaprop.overseeding_interval: All other turf species must be overseeded after a 21-day waiting period.

isoxaben.note: Pre-emergent broadleaf herbicide for established turf and ornamentals.
isoxaben.retreatment_interval: Do not repeat applications sooner than 60 days after a previous application of Gallery SC.
isoxaben.irrigation_guidance: Do not apply Gallery SC through any type of irrigation system. A single rainfall or sprinkler irrigation of 0.5 inches or more after application is necessary to activate Gallery SC; if it is not activated within 21 days, erratic weed control may result.
isoxaben.rainfast: No explicit rainfast interval is stated in the structured Gallery SC label record; activation depends on at least 0.5 inch of rainfall or sprinkler irrigation after application.
tank_mix_guidance: If the mixture balls-up, forms flakes, sludges, jels, oily films or layers, or other precipitates, it is not compatible and the tank mix combination should not be used.
Users must follow the most restrictive directions for use and precautionary statements of each product in the tank mixture.
Tank Mix Compatibility Testing: A jar test is recommended prior to tank mixing to ensure compatibility of Gallery SC and other pesticides.
isoxaben.max_apps_per_year: Do not exceed 3 applications per year or a maximum yearly of 48 oz/A (1.56 lb ai/A).
isoxaben.max_rate_per_app: Do not apply more than a total of 124 fl oz/A of Gallery SC per acre within a 12-month period.
isoxaben.reseeding_interval: Apply Gallery SC to newly seeded turfgrass only after seedlings are established at the three-leaf stage, tillering, and well rooted.
isoxaben.overseeding_interval: Do not overseed established turfgrass sooner than 60 days following an application of Gallery SC.
isoxaben.application_window_notes: Pre-emergent broadleaf herbicide for established turf and ornamentals.

clopyralid.note: Selective post-emergent broadleaf herbicide for non-residential turf.
site_restrictions: Label is for non-residential turfgrass uses.
clopyralid.retreatment_interval: No single fixed retreatment interval is stated in the structured Basagran turf record. Match the sedge or broadleaf response and local label directions before reapplying.
clopyralid.irrigation_guidance: Chemigation: Do not apply this product through any type of irrigation system.
clopyralid.rainfast: Applications of Lontrel Turf and Ornamental are rainfast within 2 hours after application.
tank_mix_guidance: Use tank mixtures with herbicides from a different group if such use is permitted; where information on resistance in target weed species is available, use the less resistance-prone partner at a rate that will control the target weed(s) equally as well as the more resistance-prone partner.

Do not tank mix Lontrel Turf and Ornamental with other herbicides labeled for use in ornamental landscapes except the specialty herbicides and/or grass herbicides listed in the chart below and only on iceplant, Myoporum, red apple and other ground covers that are tolerant to Lontrel Turf and Ornamental.
Read and follow the applicable restrictions and limitations and directions for use on all product labels involved in tank mixing.
clopyralid.max_apps_per_year: Do not apply more than 1 1/3 pint per acre per year.
clopyralid.max_rate_per_app: Thorough coverage is necessary for good results; therefore, apply as a complete spray to wet foliar application, including all leaves, stems, and root collars, but do not apply more than a total of 1 1/3 pint of Lontrel Turf and Ornamental per acre.
clopyralid.reseeding_interval: Storage and Disposal Do not reseed turfgrass for 3 weeks after application.
clopyralid.overseeding_interval: Do not overseed for three weeks after application.
clopyralid.application_window_notes: For best results and to minimize spray drift, apply in a spray volume of 20 gallons or more per acre.

trifloxysulfuron_sodium.note: ALS herbicide for broadleaf, sedge, and select grassy weeds in turf.
site_restrictions: Primarily used on warm-season turf programs; verify site turf before use.
trifloxysulfuron_sodium.retreatment_interval: Wait at least 28 days before retreating the area.
trifloxysulfuron_sodium.irrigation_guidance: Chemigation: DO NOT apply this product through any type of irrigation system or equipment.
trifloxysulfuron_sodium.rainfast: Rainfastness: Monument 75WG is rainfast within 3 hours after application.
tank_mix_guidance: For products packaged in water-soluble packaging, do not tank mix with products containing Boron or mix in equipment previously used to apply a product mixture containing Boron unless the tank and spray equipment has been thoroughly cleaned.
When the tank is 3/4 full, add any tank mix partners.
Complete filling the tank, maintaining sufficient agitation at all times to ensure surface action until the spray tank mixture is uniform.
trifloxysulfuron_sodium.max_apps_per_year: DO NOT make more than 3 applications of Monument 75WG per year at the highest labeled rate (0.53 oz/A).
trifloxysulfuron_sodium.max_rate_per_app: DO NOT apply more than 0.53 oz/A (0.40 oz ai/A) in a single application.
trifloxysulfuron_sodium.reseeding_interval: For control of Poa annua in non-overseeded bermudagrass, apply Monument 75WG plus Barricade 65WG in the fall after Poa annua has germinated, but before growth has slowed.
trifloxysulfuron_sodium.overseeding_interval: For control of Poa annua in non-overseeded bermudagrass, apply Monument 75WG plus Barricade 65WG in the fall after Poa annua has germinated, but before growth has slowed.
trifloxysulfuron_sodium.application_window_notes: Apply to actively growing weeds during early stages of development for best results.

pronamide.note: Selective herbicide for annual bluegrass control and perennial ryegrass removal from warm-season turf during spring transition. Works slowly rather than as a quick burndown.
site_restrictions: Warm-season turf only: bermudagrass, centipedegrass, seashore paspalum, St. Augustinegrass, and zoysiagrass.
Avoid spray or drainage onto bentgrass greens or areas overseeded with sensitive cool-season grasses.
Do not apply to desirable bentgrasses, fescues, annual or perennial ryegrasses, perennial bluegrass, or dichondra.
pronamide.retreatment_interval: No single fixed retreatment interval is stated in the structured Kerb SC record. Follow the annual bluegrass or ryegrass-transition program timing and do not exceed 3 applications per year or 4.5 lbs ai/acre per year.
pronamide.irrigation_guidance: Do not apply through any type of irrigation system. Moisture is essential to move Kerb SC into the weed root zone; if no rainfall occurs within a day of application, make a light overhead irrigation of 0.25 to 0.5 inch within 24 hours and avoid heavy irrigations over 1 inch.
pronamide.rainfast: No explicit rainfast interval is stated in the structured Kerb SC record; rainfall or irrigation is actually needed within 24 hours to move the product into the weed root zone.
tank_mix_guidance: Keep tank mixes conservative unless the current label and your prior use support the combination.
The label carries specific cautions around pesticides, crop oil concentrates, and adjuvants; verify compatibility before mixing.
Do not apply a wetting agent for frost protection or soil penetration to greens or tees within 14 days before or after a Kerb SC application.
pronamide.max_apps_per_year: Apply no more than 3 applications of Kerb SC T&O per year and do not exceed 4.5 lbs active ingredient per acre per year.
pronamide.max_rate_per_app: Do not apply more than 3.5 pt/acre per application overall, and do not exceed 2.5 pt/acre per application on golf course greens.
pronamide.reseeding_interval: Do not apply Kerb SC to areas that are to be overseeded with susceptible cool-season grasses within 90 days of treatment unless deactivation is planned. If earlier reseeding is necessary, apply activated charcoal at 10 lb per 1000 sq ft no sooner than 14 days after Kerb SC, then reseed no sooner than 7 days after the charcoal application.
pronamide.overseeding_interval: Do not apply Kerb SC to areas that are to be overseeded with susceptible cool-season grasses within 90 days of treatment unless deactivation is planned. If earlier overseeding is necessary, follow the activated charcoal deactivation directions first.
pronamide.application_window_notes: For the most effective annual bluegrass control on golf course greens, make applications from early fall through December. For perennial ryegrass removal, apply to warm-season turf at about 50 percent green-up.

methiozolin.note: Selective Poa program for golf course turf. Slow-acting and usually works through repeated applications over time, not a quick burn-down.
site_restrictions: Golf course turf only.
Do not apply to putting greens consisting of velvet bentgrass or colonial bentgrass.
Do not apply to hybrid bermudagrass putting greens overseeded with roughstalk bluegrass.
Not for use on turf being grown for sale or other commercial use as sod.
methiozolin.rei: No explicit restricted-entry interval is stored in the structured PoaCure label record.
methiozolin.retreatment_interval: Apply on 2- to 3-week intervals. The number of applications depends on region and surface, with repeated applications used to slowly control Poa over time.
methiozolin.irrigation_guidance: Do not apply through any type of irrigation system. PoaCure activity is mainly driven by root uptake and is enhanced by 0.1 to 0.2 inches of irrigation or rainfall after spray before foliage dries; post-application irrigation is required for optimum activity, especially on fairway-height turf.
methiozolin.rainfast: Runoff of this product will be reduced by avoiding applications when rainfall or irrigation is expected within 48 hours.
tank_mix_guidance: Do not tank-mix with an adjuvant.
Do not tank-mix with other herbicides or fungicides.
During PoaCure sequences, stop using plant growth regulators other than trinexapac-ethyl until Poa control is completed.
methiozolin.max_apps_per_year: Match the regional turf table: annual maximum use ranges from 1.0 to 2.4 gallons per acre per year depending on surface and region.
methiozolin.max_rate_per_app: Do not exceed 0.2 gallons per acre (0.6 fl oz/1000 sq ft) per application on putting greens or 0.4 gallons per acre (1.2 fl oz/1000 sq ft) per application on collars, approaches, fairways, or tees.
methiozolin.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured PoaCure record. The stored label does say not to over-seed within 45 days of the last application and not to apply PoaCure within 12 weeks of over-seeding.
methiozolin.overseeding_interval: Do not over-seed within 45 days of the last application or apply PoaCure within 12 weeks of over-seeding.
methiozolin.application_window_notes: Apply when average daily temperature is between 50 and 80 F and both Poa weeds and turfgrasses are actively growing. Do not apply during heat stress, winter dormancy, or within one week of aggressive aeration.

topramezone.note: Select turf species only. Creeping bentgrass is marginally tolerant. Do not use on St. Augustinegrass or zoysiagrass.
site_restrictions: Creeping bentgrass tolerance is marginal; spot-test before broad use.
topramezone.retreatment_interval: No single fixed retreatment interval is stated in the structured Basagran turf record. Match the sedge or broadleaf response and local label directions before reapplying.
topramezone.irrigation_guidance: DO NOT apply this product through any type of irrigation system.
topramezone.rainfast: For best results, DO NOT water or irrigate for 24 hours after application.
tank_mix_guidance: Tank Mixing Information Read and follow the applicable restrictions and limitations and directions for use on all products involved in tank mixing.
Before tank mixing, a jar test is required to ensure compatibility of herbicides or other pesticides and/or additives.
Refer to manufacturer’s labels for specific use directions, precautions, restrictions, and limitations before tank mixing with Pylex.
topramezone.max_apps_per_year: DO NOT apply more than 4 fl ozs Pylex per acre per year.
topramezone.max_rate_per_app: Restrictions and Limitations DO NOT apply more than 2.0 fl ozs of Pylex per acre (0.046 fl oz per 1000 sq ft) per application.
topramezone.reseeding_interval: Delay seeding desirable turfgrass species for 3 weeks after last application in the late-summer Bermudagrass control program. In the non-selective Bermudagrass program, seeding of cool-season species can be performed immediately after application, delay seeding bentgrass for 2 weeks, and warm-season species can be seeded, sprigged, or sodded 2 weeks after application.
topramezone.overseeding_interval: Delay seeding desirable turfgrass species for 3 weeks after last application in the late-summer Bermudagrass control program. In the non-selective Bermudagrass program, seeding of cool-season species can be performed immediately after application, delay seeding bentgrass for 2 weeks, and warm-season species can be seeded, sprigged, or sodded 2 weeks after application.
topramezone.application_window_notes: For best results, weeds should be actively growing and not under stress from lack of water, excessive water, low fertil ity, mowing shock, excessive hot or cold temperatures, or injury from other herbicide applications.

bentazon.note: Post-emergent herbicide for sedges and select broadleaf weeds in turfgrass.
bentazon.retreatment_interval: No single fixed retreatment interval is stated in the structured Basagran turf record. Match the sedge or broadleaf response and local label directions before reapplying.
bentazon.irrigation_guidance: DO NOT apply this product through any type of irrigation system.
bentazon.rainfast: Rainfall or overhead sprinkler irrigation within 8 hours after application may reduce the effectiveness of Basagran T&O.
tank_mix_guidance: DO NOT apply oil concentrate in tank mix with Basagran T&O over the top of ornamentals.
Tank mix product, if used 3.
An ideal tank mix combination will be uniform.
bentazon.max_apps_per_year: Do not apply more than a total of 64 fl ozs of Basagran T&O per acre per season.
bentazon.max_rate_per_app: 3 DO NOT apply more than 32 fl ozs of Basagran T&O per acre (1.0 pound bentazon active ingredient) in a single application.
bentazon.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured Basagran label record.
bentazon.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Basagran label record.
bentazon.application_window_notes: For best broadleaf control, do not mow turfgrass within 3 days before or after application. For best sedge control, do not mow turfgrass within 5 days of application.

pinoxaden.note: Post-emergent grass herbicide for select turf species and bermudagrass programs.
site_restrictions: Creeping bentgrass and Kentucky bluegrass may be injured.
pinoxaden.retreatment_interval: Repeat lower-rate transition or suppression programs 14 to 21 days apart when the label calls for it; otherwise match retreatment timing to the target grass and bermudagrass-transition program.
pinoxaden.irrigation_guidance: DO NOT apply by air or through any type of irrigation system.
pinoxaden.rainfast: Rainfastness Manuscript is rainfast within 1 hour after application.
tank_mix_guidance: Use tank mixtures with herbicides from a different group if such use is permitted; where information on resistance in target weed species is available, use the less resistance-prone partner at a rate that will control the target weed(s) equally as well as the more resistance-prone partner.
Read and follow the applicable restrictions and limitations and directions for use on all product labels involved in tank mixing.
Users must follow the most restrictive directions for use and precautionary statements of each product in the tank mixture.
pinoxaden.max_apps_per_year: 5.0 RESTRICTIONS AND PRECAUTIONS 5.1 Use Restrictions DO NOT apply more than 19.2 fl oz of Manuscript per acre per calendar year (0.063 lb ai/A/year).
pinoxaden.max_rate_per_app: 5.0 RESTRICTIONS AND PRECAUTIONS 5.1 Use Restrictions DO NOT apply more than 19.2 fl oz of Manuscript per acre per calendar year (0.063 lb ai/A/year).
pinoxaden.reseeding_interval: Delay applications for at least 4 weeks after sprigging, seed emergence, or sodding.
pinoxaden.overseeding_interval: Use Rate (oz/acre) Application Timing Overseeded ryegrass at Transition 2.4 to 9.6 When removal can be tolerated Lowest rates will provide partial control to aid transition Thin (Bull) paspalum 9.6 to 19.2 Actively growing Low rate should be repeated at 14 to 21 days with 0.5% v/v Adigor.
pinoxaden.application_window_notes: Post-emergent grass herbicide for select turf species and bermudagrass programs.

flazasulfuron.type: post-emergent selective with some pre-emergent activity
flazasulfuron.note: Selective ALS herbicide for warm-season turf; use caution around sensitive cool-season grasses including bentgrass, ryegrass, and Poa trivialis.
site_restrictions: Applications during spring and fall transition can injure centipedegrass and seashore paspalum.
flazasulfuron.retreatment_interval: No single fixed retreatment interval is stated in the structured Katana turf record. Match the target weed, seasonal timing, and resistance-management plan before reapplying.
flazasulfuron.irrigation_guidance: Do not apply Katana Turf Herbicide through any irrigation system.
flazasulfuron.rainfast: APPLICATION INFORMATION Rainfastness: One inch of rainfall within 3 to 6 hours of application may result in reduced weed control or require reapplication.
tank_mix_guidance: Use tank mixtures with herbicides from a different group; where information on resistance in target weed species is available, use the less resistance-prone partner at a rate that will control the target weed(s) equally as well as the more resistance-prone partner.

If ALS (Group 2) resistant weed populations have been reported in your region, use the maximum rate of Katana Turf Herbicide for the labeled use and also include additional herbicides with effective modes of action in the tank mix to control the target weed population.

If a weed pest population continues to progress after treatment with this product, either discontinue use of this product, switch to another management strategy or tank mix with an herbicide with a different mode of action, if available.
flazasulfuron.max_apps_per_year: Do not apply more than 3 applications per year.
flazasulfuron.max_rate_per_app: 6.1 ALL SPECIFIED APPLICATION SITES Restrictions: Do not apply more than 3.0 oz/acre (0.047 lbs ai/acre) per application.
flazasulfuron.reseeding_interval: Allow at least 2 weeks from the last application to the time of overseeding when applied at 1.5 oz/A (0.023 lb AI/acre).
flazasulfuron.overseeding_interval: Allow at least 2 weeks from the last application to the time of overseeding when applied at 1.5 oz/A (0.023 lb AI/acre).
flazasulfuron.application_window_notes: Selective ALS herbicide for warm-season turf; use caution around sensitive cool-season grasses including bentgrass, ryegrass, and Poa trivialis.
