triclopyr_clopyralid.note: Combination auxin herbicide for established turf; use higher rates for mature or drought-stressed broadleaf weeds.
site_restrictions: Non-residential turf only. In California, New York, Oregon, and Washington turf uses are limited to golf courses.
triclopyr_clopyralid.retreatment_interval: Repeat applications, if required, should be made not less than 4 weeks apart.
triclopyr_clopyralid.irrigation_guidance: Chemigation: Do not apply this product through any type of irrigation system.
triclopyr_clopyralid.rainfast: No explicit rainfast interval is stated in the structured Q4 Plus label record.
tank_mix_guidance: Higher application volumes may be used when Confront is tank mixed with fertilizers.
triclopyr_clopyralid.max_apps_per_year: Do not use more than 4 pints of Confront per acre per year of treatment.
triclopyr_clopyralid.max_rate_per_app: 1 On bentgrass, do not apply more than 1 pint of Confront per acre (0.37 fl oz or 2.5 tsp per 1000 sq ft) unless turfgrass injury can be tolerated.
triclopyr_clopyralid.reseeding_interval: Do not reseed for three weeks after application.
triclopyr_clopyralid.overseeding_interval: For newly sodded, sprigged, or plugged areas, delay overseeding-sensitive renovation work until 3 to 4 weeks after the sodding, sprigging, or plugging operations tied to treatment timing.
triclopyr_clopyralid.application_window_notes: Weeds Controlled and Use Rate Recommendations Use the higher rates when hard to control species are prevalent, when applications are made in late summer on mature weeds, and during periods of drought stress.

quinclorac_sulfentrazone_2_4_d_dicamba.note: Combination broadleaf and grassy weed herbicide; temporary yellowing may occur on fine fescue, bermudagrass, and zoysiagrass.
site_restrictions: Do not use on golf greens or collars. Do not use where desirable clovers or legumes are present.
quinclorac_sulfentrazone_2_4_d_dicamba.retreatment_interval: The maximum number of broadcast applications on ornamental turf and non-cropland areas is limited to 2 per year with a minimum of 30 days between applications.
quinclorac_sulfentrazone_2_4_d_dicamba.irrigation_guidance: Chemigation: Do not apply this product through any type of irrigation system.
quinclorac_sulfentrazone_2_4_d_dicamba.rainfast: No explicit rainfast interval is stated in the structured Q4 Plus label record.
tank_mix_guidance: Use tank mixtures with herbicides from a different group if such use is permitted; where information on resistance in target weed species is available, use the less resistance-prone partner at a rate that will control the target weed(s) equally as well as the more resistance-prone partner.
Certain spray tank additives (adjuvants, wetting agents, surfactants), liquid fertilizers, and tank mixtures containing emulsifiable concentrates may reduce the selectivity on the turfgrass.
Use adjuvants and spray additives or tank-mix combinations only when your experience indicates that the tank mixture will not result in objectionable turf injury.
quinclorac_sulfentrazone_2_4_d_dicamba.max_apps_per_year: The maximum annual application rate for Q4 Plus Turf Herbicide for Grassy & Broadleaf Weeds is 16 pints of product per acre per year.
quinclorac_sulfentrazone_2_4_d_dicamba.max_rate_per_app: Use Restrictions The maximum single application rate for Q4 Plus Turf Herbicide for Grassy & Broadleaf Weeds is 8 pints of product per acre per application, the equivalent of 0.75 lb quinclorac ai, 0.06 lb sulfentrazone ai, 0.88 lb 2,4-D ae, and 0.10 lb dicamba ae per acre per application.
quinclorac_sulfentrazone_2_4_d_dicamba.reseeding_interval: For newly sodded, sprigged, or plugged areas: The application of this product to newly sodded, sprigged, or plugged grasses should be delayed until 3 to 4 weeks after the sodding, sprigging, or plugging operations.
quinclorac_sulfentrazone_2_4_d_dicamba.overseeding_interval: Delay overseeding-sensitive renovation work until 3 to 4 weeks after treatment on newly sodded, sprigged, or plugged turf.
quinclorac_sulfentrazone_2_4_d_dicamba.application_window_notes: For best results, do not irrigate or water the turfgrass within 24 hours after application. Delay mowing 2 days before and until 2 days after application.

abamectin.timing: Use as an early curative nematode treatment after assay thresholds are reached, and start bermudagrass mite treatments before or in the early stages of symptom development.
abamectin.note: Specialty nematicide/miticide for greens, tees, fairways, and high-level sports turf; movement into the thatch and root zone is critical.
site_restrictions: Golf course greens, tees, and fairways, plus professional and collegiate sports fields.
abamectin.rei: Do not re-enter treated areas until sprays have dried.
abamectin.retreatment_interval: Use 14- to 21-day timing on the lighter Divanem rates, 21- to 28-day timing on the heavier nematode rates, and 14-day timing for bermudagrass mite. The minimum application interval is 14 days.
abamectin.irrigation_guidance: Apply in the early morning while turf is wet with dew or pre-irrigate with 0.1 inch of water, then irrigate with at least 0.1 inch within 1 hour after application to move Divanem through the thatch. Do not apply through any irrigation system.
abamectin.rainfast: No explicit rainfast interval is stated in the structured Divanem record; the label instead emphasizes avoiding runoff, choosing periods when rain is not predicted over the next 24 hours, and irrigating the product into the target zone immediately after application.
tank_mix_guidance: For nematode programs, the label recommends combination treatments with fungicides such as Briskway, Heritage Action, Heritage Fungicide, Heritage TL, or Velista, and those partners should be watered in with Divanem.
Daconil Action can be used in the surrounding foliar fungicide program, but the label says it should be applied separately and not watered in with Divanem.
For bermudagrass mite, tank mix Divanem with a surfactant or wetting agent and water it in immediately after application.
abamectin.max_apps_per_year: Do not exceed 50 fl oz of Divanem per acre per calendar year.
abamectin.max_rate_per_app: Do not exceed 12.2 fl oz of Divanem per acre in a single application.
abamectin.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured Divanem label record.
abamectin.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Divanem label record.
abamectin.application_window_notes: Apply to wet turf and begin watering-in before spray droplets dry. Spot-treat curative nematode areas after assay confirmation rather than waiting for severe decline.

chlorantraniliprole.timing: Apply before egg hatch; irrigate after application to move product into the soil.
chlorantraniliprole.retreatment_interval: Wait a minimum of 7 days to retreat.
chlorantraniliprole.note: Granular Acelepryn label on file; turf caterpillar table includes armyworms, cutworms, and sod worms.
chlorantraniliprole.irrigation_guidance: Do not apply Acelepryn G to saturated soil. Irrigate turf immediately after application, or allow rainfall to move the product into the soil.
chlorantraniliprole.rainfast: Runoff of this product will be reduced by avoiding applications when rainfall is forecasted to occur within 48 hours.
tank_mix_guidance: No explicit tank-mix guidance is stated in the structured Acelepryn G record.
Use the granular product alone unless the current label and your own spreader program clearly support a companion application.
chlorantraniliprole.max_apps_per_year: Do not apply more than 250 pounds of product per acre per year in broadcast applications to turfgrass.
chlorantraniliprole.max_rate_per_app: Do not apply more than 100 pounds of product per acre in a single application on sod farms.
chlorantraniliprole.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured Acelepryn label record.
chlorantraniliprole.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Acelepryn label record.
chlorantraniliprole.application_window_notes: Apply before egg hatch; irrigate after application to move product into the soil.

imidacloprid.note: Systemic neonicotinoid; chinch bug control is suppression-only on the stored Merit 2F label.
site_restrictions: Water in after application per stored KB note.
Pollinator concern per stored KB note.
imidacloprid.retreatment_interval: No fixed retreatment interval is stated in the structured Merit record; follow the annual maximum and target-pest timing on the label.
imidacloprid.irrigation_guidance: RESTRICTION: Do not apply through any irrigation system.
imidacloprid.rainfast: For optimum control, if rainfall does not occur within 24 hours after application, irrigate to move the active ingredient through the thatch.
tank_mix_guidance: Merit 2F Insecticide has been found to be compatible with commonly used fungicides, miticides, liquid fertilizers, and other commonly used insecticides.
Check physical compatibility using the correct proportion of products in a small jar test if local experience is unavailable.
imidacloprid.max_apps_per_year: Do not apply more than 1.6 pt (0.4 lb of active ingredient) per acre per year.
imidacloprid.max_rate_per_app: RESTRICTION: Do not apply more than a total of 1.6 pints (0.4 lb of active ingredient) per acre per year.
imidacloprid.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured Merit label record.
imidacloprid.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Merit label record.
imidacloprid.application_window_notes: Do not mow turf or lawn area until after sufficient irrigation or rainfall has occurred so that uniformity of application will not be affected.

trichlorfon.rei: No explicit restricted-entry interval is stored in the structured Dylox label record.
trichlorfon.retreatment_interval: Limit the number of applications to turf to 3 per calendar year with minimum retreatment intervals of 7 days.
trichlorfon.irrigation_guidance: Post-application watering-in is mandatory. Water in the product on the same day with sufficient water to thoroughly wet the remaining thatch and move the product to the root zone; if sufficient rainfall occurs after application but before watering-in, additional watering-in is not required.
trichlorfon.rainfast: For best results, avoid rain or irrigation runoff for 24 hours after application so the treatment is not washed off the area before it works in.
tank_mix_guidance: No explicit tank-mix guidance is stored in the structured Dylox label record.
If you plan to tank-mix Dylox, verify physical compatibility and follow the most restrictive label in the mix.
trichlorfon.max_apps_per_year: Do not make more than 3 applications per calendar year.
trichlorfon.max_rate_per_app: No explicit single-application maximum is stated in the structured Dylox label record beyond the labeled use-rate directions and annual application-count limit.
trichlorfon.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured Dylox label record.
trichlorfon.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Dylox label record.
trichlorfon.application_window_notes: Apply when pests are present; avoid applications during rain, and do not enter or allow others to enter treated areas until the surface is dry after watering-in.

bifenthrin.note: Do NOT water in for surface feeders. NOT effective for disease control.
site_restrictions: Do not water in when targeting surface feeders per stored KB note.
bifenthrin.retreatment_interval: No single fixed retreatment interval is stated in the structured Talstar label record. Match retreatment timing to the target pest, site, and annual-use cap before reapplying.
bifenthrin.irrigation_guidance: Do not apply this product through any kind of irrigation system. For low volume applications under 2 gallons per 1000 square feet, immediately irrigate treated area with at least 0.25 inches of water following application to ensure efficacy of sub-surface pests such as mole crickets.
bifenthrin.rainfast: Do not apply if rain is expected within 24 hours (or whatever time is necessary for the spray to dry).
tank_mix_guidance: When tank mixing Talstar Select Insecticide with other products, observe all precautions and limitations on each separate product label.

Any tank mixture which has not been previously tested should be prepared on a small scale (pint or quart jar), using the proper proportions of chemicals and water to ensure the physical compatibility of the mixture.

The following procedure is recommended for preparation of a new tank mix, unless specified otherwise in label directions: (1) Add wettable powders to tank water, (2) Agitate, (3) Add liquids and flowables, (4) Agitate, (5) Add emulsifiable concentrates, and (6) Agitate.
bifenthrin.max_apps_per_year: Maximum rates: Do not apply more than 0.2 lb ai/acre (40 fl oz of Talstar Select Insecticide) in a single application or per year for outdoor applications.
bifenthrin.max_rate_per_app: Maximum rates: Do not apply more than 0.2 lb ai/acre (40 fl oz of Talstar Select Insecticide) in a single application or per year for outdoor applications.
bifenthrin.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured Talstar label record.
bifenthrin.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Talstar label record.
bifenthrin.application_window_notes: For armyworms, cutworms, and sod webworms, delay watering (irrigation) or mowing for 24 hours after application.

clothianidin.retreatment_interval: No single fixed retreatment interval is stated in the structured Arena turf record. Match the pest timing and pressure before reapplying.
clothianidin.irrigation_guidance: If making broadcast applications, water in with enough irrigation or rainfall to move the product into the zone where insects are active.
clothianidin.rainfast: No explicit rainfast interval is stated in the structured Arena label record.
tank_mix_guidance: Use tank mixtures with insecticides from a different group that are equally effective on the target pest when such use is permitted.
8 Arena 50 WDG Insecticide1 can be tank mixed.
However, all plant species and their varieties and cultivars have not been tested with possible tank mix combinations, sequential pesticide treatments and adjuvants and surfactants.
clothianidin.max_apps_per_year: For turf, do not apply more than 0.4 lb active ingredient clothianidin per acre per calendar year (12.8 oz per acre per year).
clothianidin.max_rate_per_app: Regardless of the application method, do not apply more than 0.4 lb active ingredient clothianidin per acre per year for turf, landscape, ornamentals and non-bearing fruits and nuts.
clothianidin.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured Arena label record.
clothianidin.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Arena label record.
clothianidin.application_window_notes: Only apply after all flower petals have fallen off.

lambda_cyhalothrin.note: Pyrethroid contact insecticide for turf and ornamentals.
site_restrictions: Restricted use pesticide in the label text; verify local use limitations.
lambda_cyhalothrin.retreatment_interval: Apply at 7-day intervals if treatment is necessary.
lambda_cyhalothrin.irrigation_guidance: Do not apply this product through any type of irrigation system.
lambda_cyhalothrin.rainfast: Applying in calm weather when rain is not predicted for the next 24 hours will help keep the treatment on the target area.
tank_mix_guidance: Compatibility Scimitar GC has been found to be compatible with most commonly used fungicides, miticides, liquid fertilizers, and other insecticides.
lambda_cyhalothrin.max_apps_per_year: Do not apply more than 0.36 lb of active ingredient (52.4 fl oz of concentrate) per acre per year.
lambda_cyhalothrin.max_rate_per_app: Do not apply more than 0.36 lb of the active ingredient (52.4 fl oz of concentrate) per acre per year.
lambda_cyhalothrin.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured Scimitar label record.
lambda_cyhalothrin.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Scimitar label record.
lambda_cyhalothrin.application_window_notes: For surface insect control, delay watering or mowing for 12-24 hours for optimum control. For thatch-inhabiting insects, lightly irrigate after application with up to 1/2 inch of water. For subsurface insects, water in immediately after application with 1/4-1/2 inch of water.

cyantraniliprole.note: Anthranilic diamide insecticide with foliar and root uptake; one label table covers annual bluegrass weevil, grubs, billbugs, and turf caterpillars.
site_restrictions: Label text includes additional local restrictions in parts of New York State.
cyantraniliprole.retreatment_interval: Wait a minimum of 7 days to retreat.
cyantraniliprole.irrigation_guidance: DO NOT apply this product through any type of irrigation system.
cyantraniliprole.rainfast: Runoff of this product will be reduced by avoiding applications when rainfall is forecasted to occur within 48 hours.
tank_mix_guidance: Tank Mixtures 2 3 Ference may be tank-mixed with other pesticides.
Always follow the tank mix instructions of the product label that is most restrictive.
cyantraniliprole.max_apps_per_year: **Do not apply more than 32 fl oz of product per acre per calendar year.
cyantraniliprole.max_rate_per_app: DO NOT apply more than 32 fluid ounces (equivalent to 0.4 lb of active ingredient) of product per acre per year.
cyantraniliprole.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured Ference label record.
cyantraniliprole.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Ference label record.
cyantraniliprole.application_window_notes: To ensure optimum control, delay watering (irrigation) or mowing for 24 hours after application. Irrigate immediately after application or allow rainfall to move the product into the soil when targeting grubs.

indoxacarb.note: Oxadiazine insecticide for lepidopterous larvae and annual bluegrass weevil larvae; delay mowing and irrigation for 24 hours after application unless the label directs otherwise.
site_restrictions: Do not apply on sod farms.
indoxacarb.rei: No explicit restricted-entry interval is stored in the structured Provaunt WDG label record.
indoxacarb.retreatment_interval: Wait a minimum of 7 days to retreat.
indoxacarb.irrigation_guidance: For best results, apply in the morning when turf is wet or pre-wet with irrigation and water in immediately after application with 1/8 inch of irrigation.
indoxacarb.rainfast: Runoff of this product will be reduced by avoiding applications when rainfall is forecasted to occur within 24 hours.
tank_mix_guidance: Use tank mixtures with insecticides from a different group that are equally effective on the target pest when such use is permitted.
If tank mixing Provaunt WDG with other products, see the Tank-Mixtures section below.
4.3.4 PROVAUNT WDG IN TANK MIXTURES Add different formulation types in the sequence indicated below.
indoxacarb.max_apps_per_year: Do not apply more than 36 ounces of Provaunt WDG per acre per year.
indoxacarb.max_rate_per_app: DO NOT apply more than 36 ounces (equivalent to 0.45 lb of active ingredient) of product per acre per season in broadcast applications.
indoxacarb.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured Provaunt WDG label record.
indoxacarb.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Provaunt WDG label record.
indoxacarb.application_window_notes: For best results, apply in the morning when turf is wet or pre-wet with irrigation. To ensure optimum control of lepidopterous larvae, delay watering (irrigation) and mowing for 24 hours after application unless instructed otherwise.

spinosad.note: Delay watering or mowing for 12 to 24 hours after turf applications for best results.
site_restrictions: Do not exceed six applications per calendar year on turfgrass.
spinosad.retreatment_interval: No single fixed turf retreatment interval is stated in the structured Conserve SC record. Match the labeled pest timing and pressure before reapplying.
spinosad.irrigation_guidance: Do not apply to the above listed crop(s) through any other type of irrigation system.
spinosad.rainfast: No explicit rainfast interval is stated in the structured Conserve SC label record; the stored turf note says to delay watering or mowing for 12 to 24 hours after application for best results.
tank_mix_guidance: Avoid using less than labeled rates of any insecticide when applied alone or in tank mixtures.
If Conserve SC is tank mixed with any insecticide that reduces its selectivity in preserving beneficial insects, the full benefit of Conserve SC in an IPM program may be reduced.

Conserve SC - Tank Mix: When tank mixing Conserve SC with other materials, conduct a compatibility test (jar test) using relative proportions of tank mix ingredients prior to mixing ingredients in the spray tank.
spinosad.max_apps_per_year: Maximum Number of Applications: Do not make more than six applications per calendar year.
spinosad.max_rate_per_app: The stored Conserve SC turf table tops out at 1.2 fl oz per 1000 sq ft per application.
spinosad.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured Conserve SC label record.
spinosad.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Conserve SC label record.
spinosad.application_window_notes: For best results, apply in cool weather, 65 to 85°F, or in early morning or late evening hours.

indoxacarb_bait.note: Granular bait for broadcast or individual mound treatment; do not combine with fertilizer or other pesticides in the spreader.
site_restrictions: Do not exceed 6 lb product/acre per year.
indoxacarb_bait.retreatment_interval: Minimum application interval is 12 weeks under typical use conditions; if rainfall or irrigation occurs within 2 to 3 hours of treatment, repeat application may be needed within 7 days.
indoxacarb_bait.irrigation_guidance: DO NOT apply this product through any type of irrigation system.
indoxacarb_bait.rainfast: Runoff of this product will be reduced by avoiding applications when rainfall is likely to occur within 24 hours.
tank_mix_guidance: Do not combine Advion Fire Ant Bait with fertilizer or other pesticides in the spreader.
No broader spray-tank mix guidance is stated in the structured Advion Fire Ant Bait record because this product is a bait formulation.
indoxacarb_bait.max_apps_per_year: Do not exceed 6 total pounds of product applied per acre per year.
indoxacarb_bait.max_rate_per_app: Maximum Single Application Rate: 1.5 lb/A/application (0.00068 lb ai/A of indoxacarb-containing products as a bait formulation) 3.
indoxacarb_bait.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured Advion Fire Ant Bait label record.
indoxacarb_bait.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Advion Fire Ant Bait label record.
indoxacarb_bait.application_window_notes: Do not apply if the area to be treated is wet with dew, irrigation, or rainfall. Rainfall or irrigation within 2 to 3 hours after application may reduce effectiveness.

trinexapac_ethyl.note: Primary PGR for growth regulation. NOT a fungicide or herbicide.
trinexapac_ethyl.irrigation_guidance: Primo MAXX is rainfast from rainfall or irrigation after one hour. Watering-in is not necessary for activation, and the label says not to apply it through any type of irrigation system.
tank_mix_guidance: Primo MAXX may be tank mixed with many commonly used pesticides and liquid fertilizers.
Always check tank-mix compatibility with a jar test before mixing in the spray tank.
Do not mix Primo MAXX with any product that prohibits such mixing, and follow the most restrictive label precautions and limitations.
trinexapac_ethyl.retreatment_interval: No single fixed retreatment interval is stated in the structured Primo MAXX record. Match the program interval to clipping yield, growth rate, and the surface-management goal before reapplying.
trinexapac_ethyl.rainfast: Primo MAXX reaches the growing point by foliar uptake and is rainfast from rainfall or irrigation after one hour.
trinexapac_ethyl.max_apps_per_year: Do not apply more than 19.0 pt of Primo MAXX per acre per year; on labeled overseeding programs, do not apply more than 7.0 fl oz per 1,000 sq ft per year where specified.
trinexapac_ethyl.max_rate_per_app: No single universal per-application maximum is stored in the structured Primo MAXX record; match the surface-specific use table and do not exceed the annual maximum.
trinexapac_ethyl.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured Primo MAXX label record.
trinexapac_ethyl.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Primo MAXX label record.
trinexapac_ethyl.application_window_notes: Primary PGR for growth regulation. NOT a fungicide or herbicide.

paclobutrazol.note: Stronger Poa suppression than Primo. Affects roots and shoots.
paclobutrazol.retreatment_interval: No single fixed retreatment interval is stated in the structured Trimmit 2SC record. Match the program interval to the surface, turf response, and growth-regulation goal before reapplying.
paclobutrazol.irrigation_guidance: For best results, water in Trimmit 2SC, but not to the point of runoff, prior to rain or within 24 hours to limit surface movement.
paclobutrazol.rainfast: For best results, water in Trimmit 2SC, but not to the point of runoff, prior to rain or within 24 hours to limit surface movement.
tank_mix_guidance: Trimmit 2SC is compatible with most existing control products.
paclobutrazol.max_apps_per_year: No explicit annual maximum is stated in the structured Trimmit 2SC record.
paclobutrazol.max_rate_per_app: Do not apply more than 4 quarts per acre per year (2 lb ai/A).
paclobutrazol.reseeding_interval: When to Use for Color and Quality Enhancement of Winter Overseeded Turf and Poa annua Suppression Apply anytime after overseeded turf has successfully established itself (cool-season turf species should be near 85% of turf composition).
paclobutrazol.overseeding_interval: When to Use for Color and Quality Enhancement of Winter Overseeded Turf and Poa annua Suppression Apply anytime after overseeded turf has successfully established itself (cool-season turf species should be near 85% of turf composition).
paclobutrazol.application_window_notes: Do not apply after March 15th to putting greens to avoid delay in bermudagrass green-up.

flurprimidol.rei: No explicit restricted-entry interval is stored in the structured Cutless MEC label record.
flurprimidol.retreatment_interval: No single fixed retreatment interval is stated in the structured Cutless MEC record. Match the program interval to the surface, turf response, and growth-regulation goal before reapplying.
flurprimidol.irrigation_guidance: Chemigation: DO NOT apply through any type of irrigation system.
flurprimidol.rainfast: When tank mixing with products containing Class A PGRs, rainfall or irrigation must be delayed at least 1 hour after application or until product has dried on the leaf surface but should occur within 24 hours after application.
tank_mix_guidance: Additional turfgrass growth regulation may occur when Cutless MEC is tank mixed or used in conjunction with demethylation inhibitor (DMI) or sterol inhibiting fungicides.
Tank Mixes Cutless MEC can be tank-mixed and is compatible with most commonly-used pesticides and foliar nutrient products.
Read and follow all label directions for each tank mix product.
flurprimidol.max_apps_per_year: Cutless MEC per acre per year (2-48 applications per year over a contiguous acre).
flurprimidol.max_rate_per_app: No single universal per-application maximum is stored in the structured Cutless MEC record; match the labeled use program and do not exceed the annual maximum.
flurprimidol.reseeding_interval: For best results, delay application until 6 to 8 weeks after turfgrass sprigging or laying sod.
flurprimidol.overseeding_interval: To avoid delayed spring transition of bermudagrass, discontinue applications to overseeded turfgrasses in dormant bermudagrass stands 4 weeks prior to expected bermudagrass green-up.
flurprimidol.application_window_notes: Also avoid applications during periods of extreme disease and insect pressure.

ethephon.note: Seedhead suppression on Poa annua. Often tank-mixed with Primo.
ethephon.rei: No explicit restricted-entry interval is stored in the structured Proxy label record.
ethephon.retreatment_interval: No explicit fixed retreatment interval is stated in the structured Proxy label record; follow the labeled program timing and annual maximum for the site.
ethephon.irrigation_guidance: Do not apply PROXY GROWTH REGULATOR through any type of irrigation system.
ethephon.rainfast: PROXY GROWTH REGULATOR is rainfast within 2 hours.
tank_mix_guidance: Test tank mixes with other products on a small area before using widely.
SUCH TANK MIXTURES MAY RESULT IN FORMATION OF TOXIC FUMES.
Multiple applications of the tank mix combination may be needed.
ethephon.max_apps_per_year: Do not exceed a maximum of 30 oz of Proxy Growth Regulator per 1,000 sq ft per year.
ethephon.max_rate_per_app: No explicit single-application maximum is stated in the structured Proxy label record beyond the labeled use-rate directions and annual maximum.
ethephon.reseeding_interval: No explicit reseeding interval is stated in the structured Proxy label record.
ethephon.overseeding_interval: No explicit overseeding interval is stated in the structured Proxy label record.
ethephon.application_window_notes: For best results, apply in sufficient volume of water to provide uniform coverage.
